context_pointers.vague_reference: process in the manner defined for profile inclusion resolution
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The agent should search the codebase for where included profiles are merged or processed to understand the order and context of how variables, profiles, and includes are handled together.

context_pointers.vague_reference: order the keys according to the scope hierarchy established in the schema parser
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The agent should locate where template keys are resolved into a list or map while traversing nested scopes to observe the defined precedence and flattening rules.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_spring_property_resolution.json.description: A configuration document declares named variables that are each sourced from a key in a supplied property environment, with an optional literal fallback used when the key is absent from the environment. A declaration whose name or source key is empty or omitted is rejected and tallied. The resolved variables are interpolated into a console output pattern; the engine reports every active output target with its fully resolved pattern, followed by the number of rejected declarations.

documents.main.xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_prefix"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.prefix"/>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_body"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.body"
            defaultValue="%h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b"/>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_suffix"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.suffix"
            defaultValue=";"/>
    <springProperty name="ignored_source_is_blank" source=""/>
    <springProperty name="ignored_source_is_missing"/>
    <springProperty name="" source="logback.access.test.ignored.name-is-blank"/>
    <springProperty source="logback.access.test.ignored.name-is-missing"/>
    <appender name="console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
        <encoder>
            <pattern>${console_pattern_prefix}${console_pattern_body}${console_pattern_suffix}</pattern>
        </encoder>
    </appender>
    <appender-ref ref="console"/>
</configuration>

    </appender>

</configuration>

feature2_spring_property_via_include.jso.description: The same environment-sourced variable declarations are placed in a separate document that the main document pulls in by reference. Resolution, fallback, and rejection behave identically to the inline form, demonstrating that referenced documents participate fully in variable resolution. The engine reports each active output target with its resolved pattern and the number of rejected declarations.

documents.logback-access-test-spring.property-as-i:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<included>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_prefix"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.prefix"/>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_body"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.body"
            defaultValue="%h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b"/>
    <springProperty
            name="console_pattern_suffix"
            source="logback.access.test.console.pattern.suffix"
            defaultValue=";"/>
    <springProperty name="ignored_source_is_blank" source=""/>
    <springProperty name="ignored_source_is_missing"/>
    <springProperty name="" source="logback.access.test.ignored.name-is-blank"/>
    <springProperty source="logback.access.test.ignored.name-is-missing"/>
    <appender name="console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
        <encoder>
            <pattern>${console_pattern_prefix}${console_pattern_body}${console_pattern_suffix}</pattern>
        </encoder>
    </appender>
    <appender-ref ref="console"/>
</included>

feature3_spring_profile_selection.json.description: A configuration document gates sections behind profile expressions. A section is kept only when its expression matches the set of active profiles; a leading negation marker inverts the match, sections may nest (an inner section is evaluated only if its enclosing section is kept), and an empty expression is never kept. The engine reports, in declaration order, the output targets belonging to the kept sections together with their patterns, followed by an error count.

documents.main.xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
    <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-default-console">
        <appender name="default_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>default_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="default_console"/>
        <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-default-nested-console">
            <appender name="default_nested_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
                <encoder>
                    <pattern>default_nested_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
                </encoder>
            </appender>
            <appender-ref ref="default_nested_console"/>
        </springProfile>
    </springProfile>
    <springProfile name="logback-access-test-enable-additional-console">
        <appender name="additional_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>additional_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="additional_console"/>
        <springProfile name="logback-access-test-enable-additional-nested-console">
            <appender name="additional_nested_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
                <encoder>
                    <pattern>additional_nested_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
                </encoder>
            </appender>
            <appender-ref ref="additional_nested_console"/>
        </springProfile>
    </springProfile>
    <springProfile name="!logback-access-test-disable-empty"/>
    <springProfile name="">
        <appender name="ignored_console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>ignored_console: %h %l %u [%t] "%r" %s %b</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
        <appender-ref ref="ignored_console"/>
    </springProfile>
</configuration>

</configuration>

feature4_configuration_property_binding..description: Binds a flat map of dotted configuration keys under a fixed prefix onto a typed, immutable settings object with nested groups. Absent keys take documented defaults; supplied keys override them, including an enumerated strategy value and nested boolean/string groups. The effective value of every field is reported in a stable order, with absent optional fields rendered as a neutral null marker.

cases.expected_output:
enabled=true
config=<null>
local_port_strategy=server
tomcat.request_attributes_enabled=<null>
undertow.record_request_start_time=true
tee_filter.enabled=false
tee_filter.includes=<null>
tee_filter.excludes=<null>

enabled=false
config=classpath:logback-access-test.xml
local_port_strategy=server
tomcat.request_attributes_enabled=true
undertow.record_request_start_time=false
tee_filter.enabled=false
tee_filter.includes=example.com
tee_filter.excludes=localhost

_constraints.input_condition: configuration request contains 'mode' field
_constraints.output_rule: The execution adapter must parse the 'mode' field; only 'configure' and 'bind' are valid dispatch actions. Other values are rejected or ignored.

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'configure'
_constraints.output_rule: The engine must resolve variables, evaluate profiles, process includes, and apply typed bindings to the root object.

_constraints.input_condition: A springProperty element has a name and a non-empty 'source' attribute
_constraints.output_rule: The value is looked up in the properties map using the 'source' dotted key. If the key exists, its value wins. If not, the 'defaultValue' attribute (if present and non-blank) is used.

_constraints.input_condition: A springProperty element has a name AND a non-empty 'source' attribute, BUT the properties map key does not exist AND 'defaultValue' is empty or missing
_constraints.output_rule: The variable name is counted as an error. The variable does NOT exist.

_constraints.input_condition: An XML pattern string contains a token matching '${variableName}'
_constraints.output_rule: The engine substitutes the token with the resolved variable value from the current scope.

_constraints.input_condition: A springProperty element (inline or included) has an empty string for 'name'
_constraints.output_rule: The declaration is rejected and tallied as an error. No variable is bound.

_constraints.input_condition: A springProperty element is missing the 'name' attribute entirely
_constraints.output_rule: The declaration is rejected and tallied as an error. The input is treated as missing a required attribute.

_constraints.input_condition: A springProperty element has a non-empty 'name' but the 'source' attribute is empty (e.g., source="")
_constraints.output_rule: The declaration is rejected and tallied as an error.

_constraints.input_condition: A springProperty element has a non-empty 'name' but the 'source' attribute is missing entirely
_constraints.output_rule: The declaration is rejected and tallied as an error.

_constraints.input_condition: A document contains an <include resource="..."/> tag or similar reference
_constraints.output_rule: The engine retrieves the content of the referenced document from the 'documents' input map and joins it to the logical tree of the entry document.

_constraints.input_condition: A springProfile element has a 'name' attribute and a 'profiles' array of active profiles is supplied
_constraints.output_rule: The block is KEPT if the index of the 'name' inside the array (ignoring '!' prefix) is present in the supplied 'profiles' list. The '!' prefix indicates a negation match.

_constraints.input_condition: A springProfile element has a 'name' starting with '!'
_constraints.output_rule: The block is KEPT if the matching profile WITHOUT the '!' prefix is NOT present in the active profiles list. If the matching profile (without '!') is present, the block is DROPPED.

_constraints.input_condition: An inner springProfile is within a springProfile block
_constraints.output_rule: The inner block is only evaluated if the enclosing block was KEPT. If the enclosing block was DROPPED, inner blocks are dropped without further evaluation.

_constraints.input_candidate: A springProfile element has a 'name' attribute
_constraints.output_rule: The contents of the block are processed (variables resolved, etc.) only if the block is kept. If dropped, inner declarations do NOT exist in the output.

_constraints.input_condition: A springProfile element has an empty string for 'name' (or is empty)
_constraints.output_rule: The block is ALWAYS DROPPED. The content is invisible to the output.

_constraints.input_condition: finish processing a 'configure' request
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain a line starting with 'errors=' followed by the integer count of rejected variable declarations.

_constraints.input_condition: Multiple active appenders exist after resolution
_constraints.output_rule: Output lines for 'appender=...' must appear in the order of declaration in the XML tree.

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'bind'
_constraints.output_rule: The engine ignores XML documents, processes the 'properties' map based on a fixed schema, and outputs flat key-value pairs.

_constraints.input_condition: A setting key exists in the 'properties' map
_constraints.output_rule: The value from 'properties' overrides the default defined in the static schema.

_constraints.input_condition: A setting key does NOT exist in the 'properties' map
_constraints.output_rule: The default value from the static schema is used for output.

_constraints.input_condition: A setting is optional and absent in properties and absent/dummy in default
_constraints.output_rule: Output the literal string '<null>'.

_constraints.input_condition: Any 'bind' request
_constraints.output_rule: Output lines must appear in a stable, predefined order matching a list of top-level and nested groups.

_constraints.input_condition: Configuration declares variables for prefix, body, suffix
_constraints.output_rule: Console appender pattern must be exactly '${prefix}${body}${suffix}'. Replacements occur in the engine before output generation.

_constraints.input_condition: A 'properties' key is a dotted path like 'a.b.c'
_constraints.output_rule: This entire string is treated as a literal key name. Attribute names are NOT dot-separated unless explicitly provided in the text file but the engine treats the input as a flat map.

_constraints.input_condition: Any reject
_constraints.output_rule: Final output line is exactly 'errors=COUNT' where COUNT is a non-negative integer.

hide.replacement: a specific string accepted by the dispatch logic
a specific error marker string used for optional missing values
